meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Setup to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Overcome a lack of design skills with this intermediate Claude Code setup guide. Learn to automate UI generation using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: You don't need a design degree to build beautiful apps; you need an AI-driven workflow. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you can transform functional requirements into production-ready UI components instantly. This checklist guides you through setting up your environment to ship professional-grade interfaces without writing custom CSS from scratch.
content.intro:
You have the logic, the database, and the vision, but your UI looks like a relic from 1995. The 'no design skills' barrier is the silent killer of student projects, turning promising prototypes into abandoned repositories. It is time to stop struggling with margins and padding and start treating design as a code-generation problem.

By leveraging Claude Code in tandem with Antigravity, you move from being a 'developer who can't design' to an 'AI architect who ships.' This workflow allows you to maintain full control over your logic while offloading the aesthetic heavy lifting to LLMs that understand modern design systems.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Environment
steps.description: Initialize your project directory with Claude Code to enable deep codebase analysis and automated component generation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize an Antigravity project structure compatible with React/Tailwind components and set the design system to a minimalist, modern aesthetic.

steps.title: Define UI Component Schema
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define the functional requirements of your UI, ensuring the AI understands the layout constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a JSON schema for a responsive dashboard sidebar and main content area using Tailwind CSS utility classes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a component schema for a dashboard layout based on the provided project requirements.'

steps.title: Generate Design-Ready Code
steps.description: Prompt Claude Code to generate the actual UI code based on your Antigravity-defined schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-fidelity React component for a data-table with search, filtering, and pagination.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the component defined in components/layout.json using Tailwind CSS and Lucide icons.'

steps.title: Iterative Refinement via CLI
steps.description: Use Claude Code's terminal feedback loop to fix layout bugs and refine visual hierarchy without manual CSS edits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the CSS output for spacing inconsistencies and suggest a fix to align with 8px grid standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude chat 'Improve the spacing and visual hierarchy of the navbar component to be more professional.'

steps.title: Systematic Design Token Injection
steps.description: Inject a cohesive color palette and typography system into your codebase to unify the AI-generated components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply a consistent color palette (primary: indigo-600, surface: slate-50) across all generated components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update all component styles to use the new design tokens defined in the tailwind.config.js.'

pro_tips: Always define a global 'design-system.md' file in your root so Claude Code has a single source of truth for your UI style.
Use Antigravity to generate unit tests for your UI components immediately after creation to ensure they don't break during refactoring.
Ask Claude Code to 'critique the visual accessibility' of your generated components to ensure you meet WCAG standards automatically.
Leverage the `--cache` flag in Claude Code to speed up repetitive UI generation tasks when iterating on complex forms.
When the AI generates a 'bloated' component, use the 'refactor' command to specifically ask for the 'DRY-est possible implementation'.
content.next_steps: Once your UI is functional, deploy to Vercel and run a Lighthouse audit. Use the feedback from the audit as a prompt for Claude Code to perform final optimizations.
content.conversion_block: You now have the power to build professional-grade UIs that rival senior-level output. Stop letting design gaps hold your engineering talent hostage and start shipping the products you were meant to build. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
